file_name: WOMENSGOLFUNLIMITEDINC_03_29_2000-EX-10.13-ENDORSEMENT AGREEMENT - Intellectual Property Rights                 Confidentiality and Non-Use Obligations Agreement.txt

perturbation.original_text: The rights and obligations of the parties set forth in Exhibit A shall survive the termination or expiration of this endorsement agreement, regardless of cause or circumstances of the termination or expiration.
perturbation.changed_text: The rights and obligations of the parties set forth in Exhibit A shall survive the termination or expiration of this endorsement agreement, regardless of cause or circumstances of the termination or expiration, except for the obligations related to Intellectual Property, which shall terminate immediately upon expiration.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by stating that all rights and obligations in Exhibit A survive termination, but then creates an exception for Intellectual Property obligations, which now terminate immediately upon expiration. This contradicts the initial statement that all rights and obligations survive.
perturbation.contradicted_text: INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY RIGHTS, CONFIDENTIALITY AND NON-USE. The Professional acknowledges her obligations under the provisions of the Intellectual Property Rights Confidentiality and Non-Use Obligations Agreement attached hereto as "Exhibit A" and made a part hereof by this reference. The rights and obligations of the parties set forth in Exhibit A shall survive the termination or expiration of this endorsement agreement, regardless of cause or circumstances of the termination or expiration, except for the obligations related to Intellectual Property, which shall terminate immediately upon expiration. IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties have executed this Agreement as of the date first above written.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that all rights and obligations in Exhibit A survive termination. The changed text introduces an exception for Intellectual Property, stating it terminates immediately upon expiration. The contradicted text reaffirms the entire agreement and Exhibit A, including the survival clause. By explicitly excluding Intellectual Property obligations from the survival clause, the changed text directly contradicts the previous statement that all rights and obligations survive, thus creating an in-text contradiction as per the provided definitions for 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: All Information, including any copies thereof, in any media, in the possession or control of Recipient and Information embodied or included in any software or data files loaded or stored on computers in the possession or control of Recipient, its agents or employees, shall be removed and returned to the Company upon demand, but no later than the completion of work for the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: All Information, including any copies thereof, in any media, in the possession or control of Recipient and Information embodied or included in any software or data files loaded or stored on computers in the possession or control of Recipient, its agents or employees, shall be removed and returned to the Company upon demand, but no later than the completion of work for the Company. Notwithstanding the foregoing, copies of training materials may be retained by the Recipient for personal use.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by stating that all Information shall be returned, but then creates an exception allowing the Recipient to retain copies of training materials. This creates ambiguity and potential disputes about what constitutes 'training materials' and what can be retained.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (d) All Information, including any copies thereof, in any media, in the possession or control of Recipient and Information embodied or included in any software or data files loaded or stored on computers in the possession or control of Recipient, its agents or employees, shall be removed and returned to the Company upon demand, but no later than the completion of work for the Company. Notwithstanding the foregoing, copies of training materials may be retained by the Recipient for personal use.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates the return of 'All Information,' but the changed text creates an exception, allowing the Recipient to retain 'copies of training materials.' This introduces an in-text contradiction because the requirement to return 'All Information' is directly undermined by the exception. The contradicted text is exactly the same as the Changed Text. It's an inconsistency in the text itself. This inconsistency regarding the return of all information creates a clear conflict, potentially leading to disputes about the definition of 'training materials' and what can be retained, thus creating uncertainty and potential unenforceability.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient hereby assigns and agrees to assign all Recipient's rights in any Intellectual Property to the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient hereby assigns and agrees to assign all Recipient's rights in any Intellectual Property to the Company, except for moral rights, which are retained by the Recipient.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by stating that the Recipient assigns all rights in Intellectual Property to the Company but then creates an exception by retaining moral rights. The explicit assignment of 'all rights' is contradicted by the unassigned retention of moral rights, especially since it's now immediately before it in the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (f) Recipient further acknowledges and agrees that all new discoveries, inventions, improvements, processes, formulae, designs, drawings, training materials, original works of authorship, photos, video tapes, electronic images, documentation, trademarks and copyrights (the "Intellectual Property"), that may be developed, conceived, or made by Recipient, alone or jointly with others during her work for the Company, shall be the exclusive property of the Company and shall be deemed a work for hire. Recipient hereby assigns and agrees to assign all Recipient's rights in any Intellectual Property to the Company, except for moral rights, which are retained by the Recipient. Recipient hereby grants to the Company power of attorney for the purpose of assigning all Recipient's rights in Intellectual Property to the Company for the purposes of filings, registrations and other formalities deemed necessary by the Company to prosecute, protect, perfect or exploit its ownership and interests in Intellectual Property. Recipient further agrees to execute, acknowledge and deliver any documentation, instruments, specifications or disclosures necessary to assign, prosecute, protect, perfect or exploit the Company ownership of Intellectual Property.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states that 'all rights' are assigned but then contradicts this by stating that moral rights are retained by the Recipient. The contradicted text repeats the claim of assigning 'all rights' but now with the same explicit exception. This creates a direct contradiction because assigning 'all rights' inherently includes moral rights, and the exception creates an inconsistency regarding the scope of the assignment, causing an Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction.
